rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 23, 2025, Line 1. PETITIONER LYFT, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, May-23-2025, Line 4. PLAINTIFF FELICITA FELIX SARAVIA V. DE MONTOYA, AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Vacate Order Compelling Jams Arbitration And Request For Sanctions Under Cal. Civ. Proc. 1281.99 In The Amount Of 13,725. Plaintiff Felicita Saravia's motion to vacate order compelling arbitration is granted and her request for monetary sanctions is granted in part. The order compelling arbitration is vacated and the case is restored to the civil active list. rulings.Case Title: ARTURO CORTEZ VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 23, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Judge Harold Kahn, who is hearing all cases in department 301 on May 23, 2025, discloses that, notwithstanding that the general counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, he believes that he is and will be fair and impartial to both sides in this case and that he is not disqualified from deciding any matters in this case. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to all seven causes of action in the second amended complaint (SAC) filed by plaintiff Arturo Cortez is overruled as to the first through fifth causes of action for alleged FEHA and Labor Code violations and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth and seventh causes of action for intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress. The Regents' argument that Texas law applies to Mr. Cortez's claims lacks merit because the SAC alleges that all of Mr. Cortez's work was performed, albeit much of it remotely, for a work site in California and that allegation must be accepted as true on a demurrer. The Regents' argument that the Labor Code claim is barred by Mr. Cortez's failure to exhaust administrative remedies lacks merit because there is no allegation in the SAC regarding administrative remedies and there has been no request for judicial notice regarding any such remedies. On a demurrer a court may only consider the allegations of the operative complaint and matters that are judicially noticeable. Mr. Cortez has adequately alleged each of his FEHA claims by alleging sufficient ultimate facts showing that he was discriminated against based on his gender, sexually harassed by a supervisor, retaliated against due to his protected activity, and the Regents failed to prevent that discrimination, sexual harassment, and retaliation. Mr. Cortez's emotional distress claims are barred by the exclusivity provision of the Workers' Compensation Act. (Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal. 2d 1, 25). Moreover, Mr. Cortez's opposition memorandum stated that he intended to dismiss his negligent infliction claim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: 33 8TH STREET, LLC ET AL VS. SWINERTON BUILDERS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 23, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT UPONOR, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendant Uponor. Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied. Uponor has failed to satisfy its burden that either of the plaintiffs agreed to arbitrate any of their claims against Uponor. As a separate and independent ground for denying the motion, even if the parties had an agreement to arbitrate, the court exercises its discretion under Code of Civil Procedure 1281.2(c) to deny arbitration. Section 1281.2(c) provides that the court may deny arbitration where "[a] party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action or special proceeding with a third party, arising out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact." There is no showing that the general contractor, subcontractors, and other supplier defendants are subject to an agreement to arbitrate. Significantly, there is no showing that any of the defendants are subject to Uponor's agreement to arbitrate, which requires arbitration in Minnesota.There is a significant possibility of conflicting rulings because one adjudicator could find that the contractors caused the construction defects while another could find that defective products caused the harm. Moreover, discovery will likely be more expeditious and efficient if all of the claims remain in one forum. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
